mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of industrial production and housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion. When INDPRO and HOUST both rise sequentially for two months, equity risk premiums contract, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active quantitative tightening, rising short-term rates initially compress equity valuations. However, if CPIAUCSL simultaneously decelerates for three consecutive months, inflation expectations anchor, allowing equities to rally over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL growth slows for three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income, proxied by sustained growth in personal savings and financial assets, increases household consumption capacity. This demand shock lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth periods do not yield statistically significant positive 5-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index exceeds its 12-month moving average by more than 2.0%, supply chain bottlenecks clear and capacity utilization rises. This operational leverage translates into higher profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in > 60% of instances where INDPRO breaches the 2.0% threshold above its trend.

mechanism_narrative: The convergence of strong employment growth and robust GDP expansion confirms a self-sustaining economic cycle. When PAYEMS rises by > 150k for two consecutive months alongside GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, equity markets price in higher terminal earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and GDPC1 signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, housing market cooling acts as a monetary policy proxy. A 10% month-over-month decline in housing starts signals effective demand destruction, which initially triggers a negative S&P 500 forward return over the next 5 trading days as rate hike fears peak.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% and HOUST drops > 10% MoM.
